sentences.grammarNote:
**¿Qué tal?** = What's up? / How's it going? — casual greeting, no verb needed.
**Soy Jeff** uses **ser** — identity (your name) is a permanent trait. Subject pronoun **yo** is dropped: just **soy**, not **yo soy**.

**Este** (masculine) agrees with **sitio** (masculine noun).
**sitio web** = website — literally 'web site'. Also common: **página web** (web page/website).
**mi** (my) is invariable for singular nouns regardless of gender: **mi sitio** · **mi página**.

sentences.base_language: I like learning languages but it is very hard
sentences.target_language: Me gusta aprender idiomas pero es muy difícil
sentences.grammarNote:
**Me gusta** is how Spanish expresses liking — literally 'it pleases me'. The infinitive **aprender** follows directly.
**idioma** (language) is masculine despite ending in -a: **el idioma** · **los idiomas**. A notable exception to the usual -a = feminine pattern.

sentences.base_language: I am not very good at speaking spanish
sentences.target_language: No soy muy bueno hablando español
sentences.grammarNote:
**Hablando** is the gerund (present participle) of **hablar**. It's used here after **bueno** to describe an ongoing activity.
**soy** uses **ser** — being good or bad at something is a defining trait, not a temporary state.

sentences.base_language: I learned with other apps and they were great too
sentences.target_language: Aprendí con otras aplicaciones y también eran geniales
sentences.grammarNote:
**Aprendí** — preterite (completed past action): I learned.
**eran** — imperfect (ongoing/background past state): they were. The imperfect is used here because being 'great' was an ongoing characteristic, not a single event.
**también** = also/too — placed after the subject or verb.

sentences.base_language: But I made this app to do more "immersion"
sentences.target_language: Pero hice esta aplicación para hacer más "inmersión"
sentences.grammarNote:
**hice** — preterite of **hacer** (highly irregular): **hice · hiciste · hizo · hicimos · hicieron**.
**para hacer** = to do/make — **para + infinitive** expresses purpose.
**esta aplicación** — **esta** (feminine) agrees with **aplicación** (feminine).

**conmigo** = with me — a special fused form of **con + mí**. The parallel form is **contigo** (with you).
**Puedes** — **poder** is o→ue stem-changing: **puedo · puedes · puede**.

sentences.base_language: I tried to make this easy to use but I may have made mistakes
sentences.target_language: Intenté hacer esto fácil de usar pero puede que haya cometido errores
sentences.grammarNote:
**Puede que haya cometido** uses the present perfect subjunctive to express possibility about a past event.
**intenté** — preterite of **intentar** (regular): I tried.
**fácil de usar** = easy to use — **de + infinitive** after adjectives of difficulty/ease.

sentences.base_language: If you want to help me, that would be cool
sentences.target_language: Si quieres ayudarme, eso sería genial
sentences.grammarNote:
**Si quieres** = if you want — the **si** (if) clause uses the present indicative for real/possible conditions.
**sería** — conditional tense of **ser**: would be. The conditional is used in the result clause.
**ayudarme** = to help me — pronoun attached to the infinitive.

sentences.base_language: If you like this website, please share it
sentences.target_language: Si te gusta este sitio web, por favor compártelo
sentences.grammarNote:
**te gusta** = you like it (literally: it pleases you) — **gustar** uses indirect object pronouns: **me, te, le, nos, les**.
**compártelo** — imperative of **compartir** (to share) + direct object pronoun **lo** (it) attached to the end.

sentences.base_language: If you know other languages, you can make lessons!
sentences.target_language: Si sabes otros idiomas, ¡puedes crear lecciones!
sentences.grammarNote:
**sabes** — **saber** for knowledge/skills. Compare **conoces** (to know people/places).
**otros idiomas** = other languages — **otros** (masc. pl.) agrees with **idiomas**.

sentences.base_language: If you can't help that is OK
sentences.target_language: Si no puedes ayudar, está bien
sentences.grammarNote:
**está bien** uses **estar** — 'okay/fine' is a temporary state.
**Si no puedes** = if you can't — the negative **no** comes before the verb in Spanish.

sentences.base_language: I am happy you are learning with me either way
sentences.target_language: Estoy feliz de que estés aprendiendo conmigo de todas formas
sentences.grammarNote:
**Estés aprendiendo** is the present subjunctive + gerund, triggered by the expression of emotion **estoy feliz de que**. Any expression of emotion/desire with a change of subject requires the subjunctive: **estoy feliz de que estés** (not *que estás*).
**de todas formas** = either way / in any case.
